channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government protested Sunday the latest American announcements of military sales and assistance to Taiwan, warning the United States that it is “playing with fire.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Winter is hitting Gaza and many Palestinians have little protection from the cold
item.description: Nearly two million displaced Palestinians in Gaza are struggling to protect themselves from the coming winter.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump: Panama will lose control of Canal if it continues to ‘rip-off’ US
item.description: President-elect Trump took issue with fees charged to use the Panama Canal, a vital shipping route, on Saturday night, claiming that he will demand oversight of the waterway be given back to the U.S. when he returns to office if it's not changed.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Biden offers condolences, cooperation with Germany after Christmas market attack
item.description: President Biden issued his condolences on Saturday over an attack on a Christmas market in Germany that left five dead and injured 200. “No community—and no family—should have to endure such a despicable and dark event, especially just days before a holiday of joy and peace,” Biden wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: Officials say five people have been killed, including a 9-year-old, after a Saudi doctor drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — One week after its worst cyclone in nearly a century, and a day after a testy presidential visit, France’s impoverished Indian Ocean territory of Mayotte is still grappling with counting the dead, restoring essential services and aiding a beleaguered population.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.”.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: The Israeli military said a rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv injuring 16 people on Saturday, and Houthi rebels later reported airstrikes in Yemen’s rebel-held capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sectarian violence in Syria has been less intense than feared since Assad's ouster
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The toppling of Bashar Assad has raised tentative hopes that Syrians might live peacefully and as equals after a half century of authoritarian rule.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: France's anti-terrorism court convicts 8 people of involvement in the 2020 beheading of a teacher
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s anti-terrorism court on Friday convicted eight people of involvement in the beheading of teacher Samuel Paty outside his school near Paris four years ago, a horrific death that shocked the country. Paty, 47, was killed by an Islamic extremist outside his school on Oct.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN and SAMUEL PETREQUIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Gaza kills at least 7 people, including 4 kids
item.description: An Israeli airstrike on a residential building in central Gaza killed at least seven Palestinians, including four children, and wounded 16 other people Friday afternoon, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power after key ally vows to topple him
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau looks set to lose power next year after NDP leader Jagmeet Singh said he would move to bring down the minority Liberal government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 dead, many injured after man drives into German Christmas market in suspected terror attack: report
item.description: A festive Christmas market in Madgeburg, Germany erupted into chaos after a car was driven into a group of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian court clears Vice Premier Salvini of illegally detaining migrants on a rescue ship in 2019
item.description: ROME (AP) — A court in Sicily Friday found Italian Vice Premier Matteo Salvini not guilty of illegally detaining 100 migrants aboard a humanitarian rescue ship in 2019, when he was interior minister.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crowds in Mayotte vent frustration with cyclone response as Macron tours devastation
item.description: MIRERENI, Mayotte (AP) — Crowds in Mayotte vented their frustration at French President Emmanuel Macron, with some booing, as he toured destruction wrought by the strongest cyclone to hit the French territory in nearly a century.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What will Syria's future look like? The answer could lie in other Arab countries' recent pasts
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Even with hopes running high, so much can go wrong when a country ousts a longtime dictator and tries to start anew. The Middle Eastern and North African nations that attempted to transition to democracy in recent years can attest to that. Now it's Syria's turn to try to get it right.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials travel to Syria in search of missing Americans, meet with rebel leaders
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in Syria making the first face-to-face contact with rebel groups that took over the country in a lightning offensive earlier this month, their mission aimed at searching for kidnapped American journalist Austin Tice and other missing Americans.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Italian artisans make around 100K bulbs for the signature light display at Singapore's Christmas Wonderland
item.description: Christmas Wonderland is a festive event that happens annually in Singapore. The festival is complete with breathtaking illuminations, activities, food and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli troops will occupy a buffer zone inside Syria for the foreseeable future
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that Israeli forces will stay in a buffer zone on the Syrian border, seized after the ouster of Syria's President Bashar Assad, until another arrangement is in place “that ensures Israel's security.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation
